
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

set.seed(42)
```

```{r}
# Patients 
# Create base patient table with demographics

patients_syn <- data.frame(
  subject_id  = 1:n_patients,
  gender      = sample(c("M", "F"), n_patients, replace = TRUE),
  anchor_age  = round(rnorm(n_patients, 60, 18)),
  anchor_year = sample(2008:2019, n_patients, replace = TRUE)
)
```

```{r}
# Assign death dates to 10% of patients

mortality_rate <- 0.10
death_idx      <- sample(1:n_patients, size = round(mortality_rate * n_patients))
start_date     <- as.Date("2010-01-01")
end_date       <- as.Date("2020-12-31")

# Use runif() over the numeric date range for uniform random sampling, then convert back to Date; route through character to avoid integer coercion
patients_syn$dod <- NA_character_
patients_syn$dod[death_idx] <- as.character(
  as.Date(
    runif(length(death_idx), as.numeric(start_date), as.numeric(end_date)),
    origin = "1970-01-01"
  )
)
patients_syn$dod <- as.Date(patients_syn$dod)
```

```{r}
# Admissions 
# Generate 1–3 admissions per patient; admission and discharge times are
# POSIXct with explicit UTC timezone to avoid system-locale surpris

race_categories <- c("WHITE", "BLACK/AFRICAN AMERICAN",
                     "HISPANIC/LATINO", "ASIAN", "OTHER")
race_probs      <- c(0.60, 0.15, 0.12, 0.08, 0.05)

admissions_syn <- map_df(patients_syn$subject_id, function(id) {
  n_adm     <- sample(1:3, 1)
   
   # Random admit times spread across ~9.5 years (3e8 seconds from 2010-01-01)
  admittime <- as.POSIXct("2010-01-01", tz = "UTC") + runif(n_adm, 0, 3e8)

    # LOS drawn from Exponential(mean = 5 days); converted from days to seconds
  dischtime <- admittime + rexp(n_adm, 1 / 5) * 86400

  data.frame(
    subject_id     = id,                               # integer, matches patients
    hadm_id        = paste0(id, "_", 1:n_adm),
    admittime      = admittime,
    dischtime      = dischtime,
    admission_type = sample(c("EMERGENCY", "ELECTIVE", "URGENT"),
                            n_adm, replace = TRUE),
    race           = sample(race_categories, n_adm,
                            replace = TRUE, prob = race_probs)
  )
})
```

```{r}
# Prescriptions 

# Iterate by row index so subject_id is copied as an integer directly from admissions_syn — avoids the type mismatch that arises from string-splitting hadm_id
drug_list <- c("Metformin", "Insulin", "Furosemide",
               "Atorvastatin", "Lisinopril", "Heparin", "Aspirin")

prescriptions_syn <- map_df(seq_len(nrow(admissions_syn)), function(i) {
  n_rx <- sample(3:7, 1)
  data.frame(
    hadm_id      = admissions_syn$hadm_id[i],
    subject_id   = admissions_syn$subject_id[i],   # integer, no string parsing
    drug         = sample(drug_list, n_rx, replace = TRUE),
    dose_val_rx  = round(rlnorm(n_rx, 2, 0.5), 1),
    dose_unit_rx = "mg"
  )
})
```

```{r}
# hospital_expire_flag (death within admission window) 
# Flag admissions where the patient died *during* that specific admission
# (dod falls within [admittime, dischtime]).
# dod is cast to POSIXct (UTC) before comparison to match the timestamp columns.
admissions_syn <- admissions_syn %>%
  left_join(patients_syn %>% select(subject_id, dod), by = "subject_id") %>%
  mutate(
    dod_posix            = as.POSIXct(dod, tz = "UTC"),
    hospital_expire_flag = if_else(
      !is.na(dod_posix) & dod_posix >= admittime & dod_posix <= dischtime,
      1L, 0L
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-dod_posix) # drop the temporary cast column
```

```{r}
write.csv(patients_syn,     "data/raw-data/patients_syn.csv",     row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(admissions_syn,   "data/raw-data/admissions_syn.csv",   row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(prescriptions_syn,"data/raw-data/prescriptions_syn.csv",row.names = FALSE)
```
